    46	void exception_direct(void)
    47	{
    48	  int irq = getipsr();
    49	
    50	  arm_ack_irq(irq);
    51	  irq_dispatch(irq, NULL);
    52	
    53	  if (g_running_tasks[this_cpu()] != this_task())
    54	    {
    55	      up_trigger_irq(NVIC_IRQ_PENDSV, 0);
    56	    }
    57	}
    58	
    59	uint32_t *arm_doirq(int irq, uint32_t *regs)
    60	{
    61	  struct tcb_s **running_task = &g_running_tasks[this_cpu()];
    62	  struct tcb_s *tcb           = *running_task;
    63	
    64	  /* This judgment proves that (*running_task)->xcp.regs
    65	   * is invalid, and we can safely overwrite it.
    66	   */
    67	
    68	  if (!(NVIC_IRQ_SVCALL == irq && regs[REG_R0] == SYS_restore_context))
    69	    {
    70	      tcb->xcp.regs = regs;
    71	    }
    72	
    73	  board_autoled_on(LED_INIRQ);
    74	#ifdef CONFIG_SUPPRESS_INTERRUPTS
    75	  PANIC();
    76	#else
    77	
    78	  /* Acknowledge the interrupt */
    79	
    80	  arm_ack_irq(irq);
    81	
    82	  if (irq == NVIC_IRQ_PENDSV)
    83	    {
    84	#ifdef CONFIG_ARCH_HIPRI_INTERRUPT
    85	      /* Dispatch the PendSV interrupt */
    86	
    87	      irq_dispatch(irq, regs);
    88	#endif
    89	#ifdef CONFIG_ENABLE_ALL_SIGNALS
    90	      if (tcb->sigdeliver)
    91	        {
    92	          /* Pendsv able to access running tcb with no critical section */
    93	
    94	          up_schedule_sigaction(tcb);
    95	        }
    96	
    97	#endif
    98	
    99	      up_irq_save();
   100	    }
   101	  else
   102	    {
   103	      irq_dispatch(irq, regs);
   104	    }
   105	
   106	  tcb = this_task();
   107	
   108	  /* Update scheduler parameters.
   109	   * The arm-m architecture svc call will trigger an interrupt,
   110	   * and the actual context switch is executed after doirq is completed,
   111	   * so only the scheduling information needs to be updated in doirq.
   112	   */
   113	
   114	  nxsched_switch_context(*running_task, tcb);
   115	
   116	  /* Record the new "running" task when context switch occurred.
   117	   * g_running_tasks[] is only used by assertion logic for reporting
   118	   * crashes.
   119	   */
   120	
   121	  *running_task = tcb;
   122	  regs = tcb->xcp.regs;
   123	#endif
   124	
   125	  board_autoled_off(LED_INIRQ);
   126	
   127	  /* (*running_task)->xcp.regs is about to become invalid
   128	   * and will be marked as NULL to avoid misusage.
   129	   */
   130	
   131	  (*running_task)->xcp.regs = NULL;
   132	  return regs;
   133	}
